title: Add PodDisruptionBudget to Keystone operator
summary: Adds PodDisruptionBudget support to the Keystone operator: a reusable EnsurePDB helper in internal/common/deployment, a buildPodDisruptionBudget function with replica-aware disruption strategy (minAvailable=1 for replicas>1, maxUnavailable=1 for replicas==1), RBAC marker and Owns watch in keystone_controller.go, call site in reconcileDeployment after EnsureService, and comprehensive unit tests covering creation, labels/selector, both disruption strategies, update-on-replica-change, deployment selector matching, and boundary values.

tests_checklist.item: EnsurePDB unit tests exist and pass (creates, updates, idempotent)
PDB integration tests exist and pass (7 tests in reconcile_deployment_test.go)
Edge case: replicas==1 uses maxUnavailable instead of minAvailable
Edge case: boundary replicas==2 uses minAvailable
PDB selector matches Deployment selector exactly

code_quality_checklist.item: Owner reference set on create only (not update)
PDB name derived from CR name (no hardcoded names)
Labels use commonLabels(), selector uses selectorLabels()
Tests follow Gomega BDD patterns with NewGomegaWithT

security_checklist.item: RBAC marker uses least-privilege verbs for PDB management
No hardcoded secrets or sensitive data
No user input passes through unsanitized

architecture_checklist.item: EnsurePDB is reusable by other operators
Call site is after EnsureService and before DeploymentReady condition
Owns watch ensures PDB changes trigger reconciliation

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code — reuses existing patterns

fail_fast_checklist.item: EnsurePDB propagates all errors to reconciler

defensive_checklist.item: Drain deadlock prevented by replica-aware strategy
PDB spec always updated to desired state on reconciliation
